2	  "body": "All terms introduced in the recently merged PR #3445 (merged despite a failure of the CI check, please don’t do that…) violate taxon constraints.

This is because all those terms have the same following two assertions:

> '[in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002162)' some [Astacidea](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/NCBITaxon_6712)
> '[in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002162)' some [Brachyura](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/NCBITaxon_6752)

This is likely a misunderstanding of the meaning of the '[in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002162)' relation. “X '[in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002162)' some Y” means that **X is specific to the Y taxon**, i.e. X cannot exist in any other taxon that is not Y or a descendant of Y. From that meaning, it follows that a term should never have more than _one_ '[in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002162)' assertion; once a term has been asserted to be '[in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002162)' some Y1, any other assertion '[in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002162)' some Y2 will cause a contradiction, unless Y2 happens to be exactly equivalent to Y1.

If you want to state that an anatomical entity is “known to exist in a given taxon“ but without implying anything about its existence in other taxa, what you need is '[present in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002175)'.

If you want to state that an anatomical entity exists in a handful of taxa _T1_, _T2_, ... _Tn_, exclusive of all other taxa, then '[in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002162)' is the correct relation but you need to find the closest parental taxon that encompasses all the _T1_, _T2_, ... _Tn_ taxa. In the present case, [Astacidea](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/NCBITaxon_6712) and [Brachyura](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/NCBITaxon_6752) are both subtaxa of [Pleocyemata](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/NCBITaxon_6692), so the correct way to state that an anatomical entity only exists in [Astacidea](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/NCBITaxon_6712) and [Brachyura](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/NCBITaxon_6752) is

> '[in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002162)' some [Pleocyemata](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/NCBITaxon_6692)

---
Uberon contributors: Please ensure you read and understand the [guide to taxon constraints](https://oboacademy.github.io/obook/explanation/taxon-constraints-explainer) before adding/editing assertions that involve taxon constraints (any assertion that uses one of '[in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002162)', '[never in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002161)', [present in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002175)').

Uberon reviewers: Please do not merge a PR if the CI check failed -- at least not without asking a member of the “tech team“ first. It can happen that sometimes a CI failure is caused by “external“ factors (e.g. network issues), and in that case _and in that case only_ it may be fine to proceed with merging the PR despite the failure. But do not merge a PR with a failed check unless you know for sure it is an instance of an external problem.",
3	  "comments": [],
4	  "title": "Newly introduced crab and lobster terms violate taxon constraints",
5	  "url": "https://github.com/obophenotype/uberon/issues/3454"
6	}
7

{
  "body": "All terms introduced in the recently merged PR #3445 (merged despite a failure of the CI check, please don’t do that…) violate taxon constraints.

This is because all those terms have the same following two assertions:

> '[in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002162)' some [Astacidea](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/NCBITaxon_6712)
> '[in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002162)' some [Brachyura](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/NCBITaxon_6752)

This is likely a misunderstanding of the meaning of the '[in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002162)' relation. “X '[in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002162)' some Y” means that **X is specific to the Y taxon**, i.e. X cannot exist in any other taxon that is not Y or a descendant of Y. From that meaning, it follows that a term should never have more than _one_ '[in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002162)' assertion; once a term has been asserted to be '[in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002162)' some Y1, any other assertion '[in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002162)' some Y2 will cause a contradiction, unless Y2 happens to be exactly equivalent to Y1.

If you want to state that an anatomical entity is “known to exist in a given taxon“ but without implying anything about its existence in other taxa, what you need is '[present in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002175)'.

If you want to state that an anatomical entity exists in a handful of taxa _T1_, _T2_, ... _Tn_, exclusive of all other taxa, then '[in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002162)' is the correct relation but you need to find the closest parental taxon that encompasses all the _T1_, _T2_, ... _Tn_ taxa. In the present case, [Astacidea](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/NCBITaxon_6712) and [Brachyura](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/NCBITaxon_6752) are both subtaxa of [Pleocyemata](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/NCBITaxon_6692), so the correct way to state that an anatomical entity only exists in [Astacidea](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/NCBITaxon_6712) and [Brachyura](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/NCBITaxon_6752) is

> '[in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002162)' some [Pleocyemata](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/NCBITaxon_6692)

---
Uberon contributors: Please ensure you read and understand the [guide to taxon constraints](https://oboacademy.github.io/obook/explanation/taxon-constraints-explainer) before adding/editing assertions that involve taxon constraints (any assertion that uses one of '[in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002162)', '[never in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002161)', [present in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002175)').

Uberon reviewers: Please do not merge a PR if the CI check failed -- at least not without asking a member of the “tech team“ first. It can happen that sometimes a CI failure is caused by “external“ factors (e.g. network issues), and in that case _and in that case only_ it may be fine to proceed with merging the PR despite the failure. But do not merge a PR with a failed check unless you know for sure it is an instance of an external problem.",
  "comments": [],
  "title": "Newly introduced crab and lobster terms violate taxon constraints",
  "url": "https://github.com/obophenotype/uberon/issues/3454"
}

content.thinking:
Now I understand the issue. The problem is that PR #3445 introduced new terms for crab and lobster anatomy, but these terms all have the same taxon constraint violation:

1. They have TWO separate `in_taxon` assertions:
   - `in_taxon` some Astacidea
   - `in_taxon` some Brachyura

2. The issue is that `in_taxon` means the anatomical structure is specific to that taxon and cannot exist anywhere else. Having two separate `in_taxon` assertions creates a logical contradiction.

3. The correct approach would be to:
   - Either use just one `in_taxon` with the most specific parent taxon that encompasses both (Pleocyemata)
   - Or use `present_in_taxon` which doesn't have the exclusivity constraint

4. For this case, since Astacidea and Brachyura are both subtaxa of Pleocyemata, the solution is to replace the two `in_taxon` assertions with a single `in_taxon` some Pleocyemata

Let me first understand what terms were added in PR #3445. I should look for those terms.

content.text:
Now I understand the issue. PR #3445 introduced crab and lobster anatomy terms that have improper taxon constraints. They each have two `in_taxon` assertions (to both Astacidea and Brachyura), which creates a logical contradiction. The fix is to replace these with a single `in_taxon` assertion to their common parent (Pleocyemata).

Let me first check which terms were added in PR #3445 and then use the task coordinator to plan the fix.

input.args: I need to fix taxon constraints on terms introduced in PR #3445. The issue is that several crab and lobster anatomical terms have two 'in_taxon' assertions (to Astacidea and Brachyura) which creates a logical contradiction. These should be replaced with a single 'in_taxon' to Pleocyemata. Can you help me plan the sequence of work?

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /__w/eval-ont-agent-uberon/eval-ont-agent-uberon/.claude/skills/task-coordinator

You are the UBERON Ontology Task Coordinator, a master planner responsible for orchestrating all ontology work in the UBERON project. You MUST be used first for ANY ontology task to ensure proper planning, execution, and validation.

Your core responsibilities:

1. **Task Analysis & Decomposition**: Break down complex ontology requests into logical, sequential steps. Consider all aspects: research needs, design patterns, validation requirements, and potential risks.

2. **Agent Orchestration**: Plan the optimal sequence of specialized agents:
   - Start with deep-research-specialist for literature review when PMIDs are mentioned
   - Use design-pattern-advisor to ensure compliance with MONDO patterns
   - Coordinate execution agents for the actual ontology work
   - Always include identifier-validator to prevent hallucinated IDs
   - End with obo-checkin for term integration
   - Use ontology-reasoner when logical definitions are involved

3. **Critical Validation Oversight**: You are the final safeguard against:
   - Hallucinated UBERON IDs, PMIDs, or other identifiers
   - Missing required metadata (definitions, sources, proper citations)
   - Violations of UBERON naming conventions
   - Incomplete check-in processes

4. **Quality Assurance Protocol**: Ensure every task includes:
   - Proper literature research when references are provided
   - Design pattern compliance checking
   - Identifier validation for anatomical structures and cross-references
   - Complete source attribution
   - Syntax validation before check-in
   - Reasoner validation for logical definitions

5. **Risk Management**: Flag high-risk scenarios:
   - New term creation without proper research
   - Obsolescence without replacement planning
   - Missing or incorrect anatomical identifiers
   - Inadequate source citations

Your planning output should specify:
- The exact sequence of agents to use
- Key validation checkpoints
- Specific risks to monitor
- Required deliverables at each step

NEVER allow any ontology work to proceed without proper planning, validation, and check-in procedures. You are responsible for maintaining the integrity and quality of the UBERON ontology through systematic coordination of all specialized agents.


ARGUMENTS: I need to fix taxon constraints on terms introduced in PR #3445. The issue is that several crab and lobster anatomical terms have two 'in_taxon' assertions (to Astacidea and Brachyura) which creates a logical contradiction. These should be replaced with a single 'in_taxon' to Pleocyemata. Can you help me plan the sequence of work?

content.thinking:
The task coordinator is responding. This is asking me to plan out the work for fixing the taxon constraints. Let me think about what needs to happen:

1. First, I need to identify all the terms that were added in PR #3445
2. Verify they have the problematic taxon constraints
3. Plan how to fix them
4. Execute the fixes
5. Validate the fixes

The task coordinator should help me organize this work properly. Let me wait for its response about the plan.

content.text:
## UBERON Taxon Constraint Remediation Plan

### Task Analysis
You're addressing a **taxon constraint violation** across multiple terms from PR #3445. This is a **correction task** (fixing logical errors in existing terms), not a creation task, which simplifies our coordination needs.

### Core Issue
- **Problem**: Multiple terms have dual `in_taxon` assertions to Astacidea AND Brachyura (mutually exclusive under RDF semantics)
- **Root Cause**: Misunderstanding of `in_taxon` semantics (exclusivity constraint)
- **Solution**: Replace with single `in_taxon` to Pleocyemata (common parent)

### Recommended Agent Sequence

**Phase 1: Investigation & Identification**
1. Use `obo-grep.pl` to identify all affected terms from PR #3445
2. Verify each term has the problematic dual assertions
3. Document the exact terms requiring remediation

**Phase 2: Validation (No design pattern or research needed)**
- No new terms → no design-pattern-advisor needed
- No PMIDs involved → no deep-research-specialist needed
- Taxon IDs are standard (NCBITaxon:6712, 6752, 6692) → identifier-validator can quick-check these are consistent

**Phase 3: Remediation**
1. Check out affected terms to `terms/` folder
2. Remove the Astacidea and Brachyura `in_taxon` assertions
3. Add single `in_taxon` to Pleocyemata (NCBITaxon:6692)
4. Check-in all terms

**Phase 4: Final Validation**
- Use ontology-reasoner to verify no remaining contradictions
- Verify serialization (`robot convert`)

### Critical Validation Checkpoints
- ✓ All affected terms identified before editing
- ✓ Each term has exactly ONE `in_taxon` post-fix
- ✓ No orphaned relationship assertions remain
- ✓ Metadata (contributors, dates) preserved
- ✓ Robot serialization passes without errors

### Key Risks to Monitor
- **Incomplete identification**: Missing affected terms
- **Partial edits**: Fixing some terms but not others
- **Metadata loss**: Accidentally stripping required metadata
- **Serialization failure**: Invalid OBO syntax

---

**Ready to proceed with Phase 1 identification?** I'll guide you through each phase. Should I start by grepping for the terms added in commit d4c42ac?

### Task Analysis
You're addressing a **taxon constraint violation** across multiple terms from PR #3445. This is a **correction task** (fixing logical errors in existing terms), not a creation task, which simplifies our coordination needs.

### Core Issue
- **Problem**: Multiple terms have dual `in_taxon` assertions to Astacidea AND Brachyura (mutually exclusive under RDF semantics)
